concerns.text: FR-009 requires a manual annotation pilot with human raters (n≥50) to validate automated features. The plan.md, research.md, and data-model.md contain no corresponding task, phase, or data entity for human-in-the-loop annotation. The 'Manual Annotation Pilot' is mentioned in research.md Section 3.4 but lacks implementation details (e.g., platform, rater recruitment, interface) and is absent from the code structure (code/) and data model. This leaves the requirement unbacked in the implementation plan.

SC-005 requires measuring total compute time against a specific GitHub Actions free-tier limit (e.g., 6 hours). The plan.md Summary states 'Total pipeline ≤ 6 hours', but SC-005 in spec.md explicitly contains a placeholder '-hour' (e.g., 'measured against the -hour GitHub Actions free-tier limit'). The plan does not resolve this missing value in the spec, nor does it define a specific success metric for this SC in the data model or contracts. The spec itself is incomplete regarding the threshold value.

SC-002 requires measuring ASR against the 'baseline ASR reported in the original MedMisBench paper'. The plan.md and research.md do not identify which specific paper or baseline value will be used for this comparison, nor do they define a data entity to store this baseline reference. Without a defined source or storage mechanism for the baseline, the success criterion cannot be measured or verified.
